schedule.description: Story 1 — Verstappen skips NLS3, targets April 18-19 qualifiers
Story 2 — Verstappen NLS2 win stripped, tyre breach
Story 2 — Bold take on the DQ and what it means
Story 3 — Antonelli youngest F1 leader ever
Story 5 — Red Bull crisis, Verstappen exit clause
Story 6 — Miami GP sprint weekend preview

stories.title: Verstappen Skips NLS3 — Nurburgring 24H Qualifiers April 18-19 Instead

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Correction: Max Verstappen is NOT at the Nurburgring today.

His #3 Mercedes GT3 is competing at Paul Ricard (GT World Challenge Europe) this weekend.

Next Nordschleife date: April 18-19 — the 24H Nurburgring qualifiers. #F1 #F1Fanrecap

stories.title: Verstappen's NLS2 Win Stripped — Tyre Rule Breach

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Max Verstappen's Nurburgring NLS2 victory has been STRIPPED.

The #3 Winward Mercedes used 7 tyre sets in the race — the limit is 6. Infraction happened in qualifying.

Victory awarded to #99 Rowe Racing BMW — Harper/Pepper. #NLS #F1Fanrecap

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Verstappen can't catch a break in 2026.

Struggling at Red Bull in F1. Now his Nurburgring win is DQ'd over a tyre counting error.

An internal mistake, according to the team. Still hurts. #F1 #F1Fanrecap

stories.title: Antonelli Leads F1 — Youngest Championship Leader in History

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Kimi Antonelli: 19 years old. Leads the F1 championship.

He became the youngest-ever title leader at Japan — breaking Lewis Hamilton's 19-year-old record set at the 2007 Spanish GP.

72 points. 9 clear of Russell. Just getting started. #F1 #F1Fanrecap

stories.title: FIA April 20 Vote — Countdown to Rule Change Decision

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
9 days until the most important vote in F1 2026.

April 20: FIA meets to decide on energy management rule changes — triggered by Bearman's 50G Japan crash.

If passed, the changes could be in place for Miami. The clock is ticking. #F1 #F1Fanrecap

stories.title: Red Bull Crisis Deepens — Verstappen Exit Clause Clock Ticks

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Max Verstappen: 9th in the F1 championship. 12 points. 60 behind Antonelli.

His Red Bull exit clause becomes active this summer — he can leave for 2027 if he's not in the top 2.

Top 2? Right now that feels impossible. #F1 #F1Fanrecap

stories.title: Miami GP Preview — Sprint Weekend, Five-Week Break, May 1-3

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
F1 returns in 20 days.

Miami Grand Prix — May 1-3 — is a sprint weekend. Antonelli will defend his championship lead. Verstappen desperately needs points.

After a 5-week break, it cannot come soon enough. #F1Miami #F1Fanrecap

stories.title: Hamilton's Ferrari Renaissance — 41 Points, Fourth in Championship

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text:
Lewis Hamilton's 2026 at Ferrari: P4 Australia, P3 China (maiden Ferrari podium), P6 Japan.

41 points. 4th in the championship. Running even with Leclerc.

The winter at Fiorano is paying off. #F1 #F1Fanrecap
